verdict.answer_first: A is most answer-first: '`lsof -i :3000` — it lists...' gives the command and result immediately; B also starts with '`lsof -i :3000`' but adds 'On macOS' first.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits more cleanly to one recommendation with '`lsof -i :3000`' and calls the fuller command 'A close second'; B is slightly more note-heavy and option-surveying.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it adds practical failure/context notes: 'Add `sudo` if...' and '`kill` handles them all at once.'
